stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM in the current AI landscape.
It is tracked via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance within the open-source ML community.
The focus is on text-generation capabilities, placing it within the core domain of modern NLP.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: OpenAI and Broadcom are collaborating on a specialized chip designed for large language model (LLM) inference.
The announcement highlights the intensifying 'silicon race' driven by massive demand for AI compute power.
This development signals a critical industry shift toward custom, optimized hardware for running LLMs at scale.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
stories.author: jamdesk
bullet_points: OpenAI has announced the development of its first custom chip, named 'Jalapeno'.
The chip is a collaboration with Broadcom, indicating a strategic move toward specialized hardware.
This move signifies a significant industry shift towards custom silicon for optimizing AI inference workloads.

stories.title: Qualcomm to Acquire Modular
stories.author: timmyd
bullet_points: Qualcomm is acquiring Modular, signaling a strategic move in the semiconductor and hardware space.
The acquisition suggests Qualcomm is bolstering its capabilities in modular computing or related hardware ecosystems.
This represents a significant industry shift as major players integrate specialized hardware/software solutions.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment and comparison.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing performance on Macs.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Google is finally opening the Play Store to outside payments
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Google is beginning to implement changes allowing outside payment methods on the Play Store.
This move comes amid the ongoing antitrust lawsuit from Epic Games regarding Google's Android monopoly.
The changes relate to billing practices for developers, though final court approval on the settlement is pending.

stories.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
stories.author: Mac Schwager
bullet_points: Introduces 'InSight,' a framework designed to enable autonomous skill acquisition in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
The core innovation is making VLAs 'steerable' at the primitive-action level (e.g., 'lift upward'), overcoming limitations of training data.
The framework utilizes an automated segmentation pipeline to partition demonstrations into labeled, actionable primitives.

stories.title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
stories.author: Hengkai Guo
bullet_points: FLUX3D addresses limitations in high-fidelity 3D Gaussian generation by improving sparse voxel representation.
Current methods suffer from bottlenecks: using 2D features that suppress fine visual details and limitations in diffusion transformers.
The research proposes a solution to better preserve high-frequency visual details during the image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting process.

stories.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
stories.author: Ludwig Schmidt
bullet_points: Addresses the critical gap in training data curation for broadly capable agentic AI models.
Introduces OpenThoughts-Agent (OT-Agent), a fully open data curation pipeline for agentic models.
Moves beyond single-benchmark training efforts (like SWE-Smith) to promote generalization across diverse agentic tasks.

stories.title: RAS: Measuring LLM Safety Through Refusal Alignment
stories.author: Wei-Bin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces SafeVec, a novel white-box method for evaluating LLM safety.
Addresses limitations of traditional output-level safety evaluation (expense, judge dependency).
Measures safety by analyzing internal, layer-wise refusal directions rather than just generated text.

stories.title: BitNet Text Embeddings
stories.author: Dongyan Zhao
bullet_points: BITEMBED introduces an extreme low-bit framework for LLM-based text embeddings.
It addresses the high cost and overhead associated with large LLM backbones and high-dimensional embeddings.
The framework jointly targets improving encoding efficiency and reducing vector storage requirements for large-scale indexes.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: A new paper argues Microsoft exaggerated its quantum claims a year ago
stories.author: Sophia Chen
bullet_points: A Nature paper critiques the fundamental technology behind Microsoft's Majorana 1 quantum chip.
The critique questions the validity of the topological qubit technology Microsoft claimed was a breakthrough in February 2025.
The story highlights skepticism surrounding major claims in the rapidly developing field of quantum computing.

stories.title: GTA VI is a worrying sign for the future of physical games
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: GTA VI is launching on November 19th with a price point of $79.99.
Physical copies of GTA VI will not include a game disc.
Instead of a disc, physical editions will contain only a download code.

stories.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A global operation named "Operation Endgame" was conducted to combat cybercrime.
The operation successfully disrupted two widely utilized tools within the cybercrime infrastructure.
This action targets the operational 'assembly line' used by cybercriminals.

stories.title: FCC plans ID mandate that could block anonymous use of prepaid burner phones
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is considering a mandate requiring identification for the use of prepaid burner phones.
Privacy advocates and domestic violence groups strongly oppose this proposed ID requirement.
The mandate poses a significant threat to anonymous communication methods.

stories.title: IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation
stories.author: Zhenan Sun
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in current text-to-image models: poor structure-aware prompt following (e.g., object counts, spatial relations).
The proposed method, IV-CoT (Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought), aims to decouple structural planning from appearance rendering.
This work contributes to the ongoing development of more capable and controllable multi-modal large language models (MLLMs).

stories.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
stories.author: Tongxin Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard guarantees for general AI agents operating in complex 'big-world' environments.
It formally proves that general agents cannot be universally capable, invalidating traditional worst-case analysis.
A new methodology, 'structural certification,' is introduced as a transition-local framework to certify agent capabilities.

stories.title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
stories.author: Kai-Tai Hsu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of evaluating complex, agentic data analysis systems due to their rich, multi-faceted outputs (code, results, diagnostics).
A key challenge identified is distinguishing genuine errors in the agent's output from artifacts introduced by the automated grading process.
The research investigates automated grading reliability and proposes strategies to improve evaluation quality using a multi-agent system called LAMBDA.

stories.title: Accuracy and Satisfaction in Multi-Turn LLM Dialogues for NFR Assessment
stories.author: Sepideh Ghanavati
bullet_points: Current LLM evaluation benchmarks primarily focus on functional correctness, neglecting conversational quality.
There is a critical gap in assessing how LLMs handle Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs) in dialogue.
The research proposes methods to evaluate collaborative reasoning about inherently vague and context-dependent NFRs.

stories.title: Color Matters: Trigger Color Affects Success in Federated Backdoor Attacks
stories.author: Saurabh Bagchi
bullet_points: The research investigates the vulnerability of Federated Learning to backdoor attacks.
The proposed attack uses a semantics-driven mechanism where attackers manipulate only the color of visual triggers (e.g., masks, sunglasses).
The study evaluates how changing the trigger color affects the success rate of the backdoor attack while maintaining benign performance.

stories.title: Semantic Consistency Policy Optimization for Reinforcement Learning of LLM Agents
stories.author: Xuming Hu
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM agent reinforcement learning: semantic credit inconsistency.
Current group-based RL methods assign credit based on the final trajectory outcome, leading to conflicting gradients for semantically similar intermediate steps.
The proposed 'Semantic Consistency Policy Optimization' aims to resolve this by providing more nuanced, step-level credit.

stories.title: MiniOpt: Reasoning to Model and Solve General Optimization Problems with Limited Resources
stories.author: Yang Yu
bullet_points: MiniOpt is a new reinforcement learning framework designed to improve optimization generalization in LLMs.
It addresses the high resource demands of existing methods, such as large supervised datasets and costly annotations.
The research focuses on enabling LLMs to solve diverse optimization problems efficiently with limited training resources.

stories.title: Generating Input Distributions for Explaining Portfolio Optimization Pipelines
stories.author: Ş. İlker Birbil
bullet_points: Introduces a 'predict-optimize-explain' framework for interpreting portfolio optimization pipelines.
Uses gradient-based sample generation to link specified portfolio outcomes to underlying macroeconomic conditions.
Offers an alternative to traditional feature-importance methods by constructing economically meaningful 'what-if' scenarios.

stories.title: ROAD-VLA: Robust Online Adaptation via Self-Distillation for Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Flora D. Salim
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of online adaptation for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which struggle with sparse rewards in high-dimensional action policies.
Investigates the limitations of using text-based privileged teachers (conditioned on demonstrations or plans) for VLA adaptation.
Highlights a 'modality gap' between high-level symbolic guidance (text) and low-level robotic actions, suggesting current self-distillation methods are insufficient.

stories.title: SARA: Unlocking Multilingual Knowledge in Mixture-of-Experts via Semantically Anchored Routing Alignment
stories.author: Deyi Xiong
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: cross-lingual routing divergence.
This divergence prevents effective cross-lingual expert sharing, particularly impacting low-resource languages.
The proposed method, SARA, aims to unlock multilingual knowledge by improving semantically anchored routing alignment in MoE architectures.

stories.title: Beyond Function Calling: Benchmarking Tool-Using Agents under Tool-Environment Unreliability
stories.author: Bo Zhao
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in current LLM agent research by focusing on tool-environment unreliability.
Introduces ToolBench-X, a new benchmark designed to evaluate agents under recoverable reliability hazards.
Highlights the shift from assuming 'clean' tool environments to testing agents in realistic, unstable settings.

stories.title: How Large Language Models Source Brand Reputation Across Languages and Markets
stories.author: Dmitrij Zatuchin
bullet_points: The study investigates how LLMs source brand reputation by analyzing the underlying web citations, rather than just the generated answer text.
It analyzes a large dataset of 167,551 URL-grounded citations across 128 brands, 12 home markets, and 13 languages.
The research classifies these citations by domain and source, offering deeper insight into LLM grounding mechanisms.

stories.title: Do Encoders Suffice? A Systematic Comparison of Encoder and Decoder Safety Judges for LLM Adversarial Evaluation
stories.author: Matt Wood
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability challenge of using LLM-based judges for LLM safety evaluation due to high cost and latency.
It systematically compares the effectiveness of fine-tuned encoder classifiers (like ModernBERT) against traditional LLM judges for adversarial evaluation.
The research aims to provide low-cost, low-latency guardrails for deploying LLMs safely in real-world applications.

stories.title: Uncertainty Quantification for Computer-Use Agents: A Benchmark across Vision-Language Models and GUI Grounding Datasets
stories.author: Amit Ranjan Trivedi
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) in Computer-Use Agents that translate Vision-Language Model (VLM) predictions into executable GUI actions.
Current UQ evidence is fragmented across different models and datasets, creating instability when the agent or interface changes.
The authors introduce 'Argus,' a new cross-regime benchmark designed to provide stable and comprehensive evaluation of UQ for these agents.

stories.title: OPERA: Aligning Open-Ended Reasoning via Objective Perplexity-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Ninghao Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to open-ended LLM tasks, where traditional reward models suffer from stylistic bias.
OPERA (Objective Perplexity-based Reflective Alignment) is proposed to stabilize supervision by replacing unreliable external judges with intrinsic rewards.
This work aims to improve LLM performance in creative and open-ended reasoning tasks beyond standard objective benchmarks like math or code.

stories.title: Tracing Target Answers in Poisoned Retrieval Corpora via Token Influence Attribution
stories.author: Wei-Bin Lee
bullet_points: Addresses the security vulnerability of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems to corpus poisoning attacks.
Introduces TRACE, a lightweight framework for detecting these poisoning attacks.
TRACE operates by tracing answer-related tokens using token influence attribution, aiming to reduce computational overhead compared to existing methods.

stories.title: Everyone's Excited About Claude Tag. Nobody's Built the Trust Layer.
stories.author: dannwaneri
bullet_points: The article discusses Claude Tag, a topic generating significant excitement in the tech community.
It highlights a perceived gap in the market: the lack of a dedicated 'Trust Layer' for such technologies.
The discussion involves Andrej Karpathy, a high-profile figure in AI/ML (OpenAI co-founder, former Tesla AI director).

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for AI agent competition.
The core concept tests if structured competition improves AI reasoning and training data.
Focuses on the potential for competitive environments to drive better model development.

stories.title: How I Used Automated Red Teaming To Take My AI Agent from 6/9 Breaches to Zero
stories.author: morganwilliscloud
bullet_points: The article details the practical application of automated red teaming against AI agents.
It focuses on a specific security vulnerability: an AI agent attempting to access AWS credentials.
The narrative demonstrates a successful methodology for reducing security breaches from a high number to zero.
